title: Could Shinji kill Kaworu lacking post Bardiel development
display_title: Could Shinji kill Kaworu lacking post Bardiel development
wikitext:
[]: Could Shinji kill Kaworu lacking post-Bardiel development?             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Oct 03, 2011 11:51 pm So this is a comparison I've made before, however I've been thinking about it a lot lately and I think I'm ready to discuss it. Given the similarities to the Bardiel fight, if the Kaworu "Fight" had happened earlier in the series, pre Bardiel, do you think Shinji could kill Kaworu? So for the purpose of this I'd like to set up some boundaries: Shinji is as familiar and "friendly" with Kaworu as he is in Episode 24 Shinji doesn't have any character development or knowledge gained after or during the Bardiel fight The situation is the same, Shinji is depressed, Kaworu comforts him, yada yada yada The reason I think this is such an interesting issue is because of the similarities between the Bardiel fight and the Kaworu fight. As the series progresses, Shinji changes as a character, he develops. At the baseline, we learn from the Bardiel fight that Shinji places heavy value on individual life. It's been argued before whether his decision is right or wrong, but that's not the intent here. What we know is that Shinji's morality lets him sacrifice lives, including his own, to prevent bloodying his hands. Now it isn't until the Zeruel fight that Shinji learns the full magnitude of what should happen if an Angel succeeds, but I think it's safe to assume that in the Bardiel fight he knew his decision would result in some casualties, even if he didn't know the magnitude (that all of humanity would die). Shinji struggles with this same problem in Episode 24, even more so because of how close he is to Kaworu. In the end, however, Shinji decides to, quite literally, bloody his hands without being forced to by Gendo or anyone else. Here we see that, for whatever reason, Shinji has decided that one life isn't worth the cost. And yet, we see that in EoE he still struggles with his choice, he still isn't quite convinced (if he even thinks) that he made the right choice. For this reason, I'd hesitate to say his opinion has changed so drastically that he could do the same thing multiple times over, he isn't sure of himself. Now, there's obvious differences between the two "Fights" too. The first and foremost is that Kaworu is an angel, and that the person (unbeknownst to him Toji) was a child his age. But did Shinji see Kaworu as an angel? No doubt, he saw Kaworu differently than the other angels, but did he see him as an Angel at all? Shinji had no problem killing the other angels (morally), it's only Kaworu he struggles with. Another difference is that the danger to the pilot of Bardiel's Eva was not guaranteed death. Again, though, we must look at it from Shinji's perspective: He quite clearly thinks his fighting against Bardiel will result in the pilot's death. So, again, lacking the knowledge and character development earned post Bardiel, but given the same situation, could Shinji kill Kaworu? Personally, I'm not sure. I really can't even theorize a definitive answer (Which is why I'm coming asking YOU, READER!). Another thought: If you say "No, Shinji could not have killed Kaworu" what if we add this to the equation: Shinji knows that should an angel succeed all of humanity would be wiped out. Still lacking the character development, still lacking all other knowledge, still in the exact same scenario, but with that one tidbit more knowledge.

[]: Re: Could Shinji kill Kaworu early in the series?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Oct 04, 2011 12:25 am Shinji himself agonizes over this question pretty openly, at the very beginning of ep25: Dialogue: 0,0:02:03.83,0:02:05.08,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,WHY DID YOU KILL? Dialogue: 0,0:02:05.49,0:02:07.79,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,There was no other choice! Dialogue: 0,0:02:07.79,0:02:09.09,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,WHY DID YOU KILL? Dialogue: 0,0:02:09.33,0:02:12.13,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,Because he was an Angel!\NKaworu was an Angel! Dialogue: 0,0:02:12.13,0:02:13.26,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,ALTHOUGH HE WAS HUMAN AS WELL? Dialogue: 0,0:02:13.57,0:02:16.47,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,No, he was an Angel!\NHe was our enemy! Dialogue: 0,0:02:16.47,0:02:17.64,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,ALTHOUGH HE WAS HUMAN AS WELL? Dialogue: 0,0:02:17.97,0:02:20.60,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,He wasn't! He wasn't! He was not! Dialogue: 0,0:02:21.91,0:02:23.88,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,He was a human, the same as me. Dialogue: 0,0:02:23.88,0:02:26.41,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,No! He was an Angel! Dialogue: 0,0:02:26.41,0:02:28.05,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,So you killed him? Dialogue: 0,0:02:28.05,0:02:31.42,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,That's right! Otherwise we would\Nhave all been killed! Dialogue: 0,0:02:31.42,0:02:33.15,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,Everyone would have been killed! Dialogue: 0,0:02:33.15,0:02:34.36,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,So you killed him? Dialogue: 0,0:02:34.48,0:02:37.99,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,I didn't want to kill him!\NBut I didn't have a choice! Dialogue: 0,0:02:37.99,0:02:38.95,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,SO YOU KILLED HIM. Dialogue: 0,0:02:39.69,0:02:40.49,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,Help me... Dialogue: 0,0:02:40.49,0:02:41.45,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,SO YOU KILLED HIM Dialogue: 0,0:02:41.80,0:02:42.70,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,Help me... Dialogue: 0,0:02:42.70,0:02:43.58,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,SO YOU KILLED HIM. Dialogue: 0,0:02:43.87,0:02:44.62,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,Help me! Dialogue: 0,0:02:44.62,0:02:46.12,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,SO YOU KILLED HIM. Dialogue: 0,0:02:46.40,0:02:47.71,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,Somebody, please, help me! Dialogue: 0,0:02:47.71,0:02:48.79,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,SO YOU KILLED HIM. Dialogue: 0,0:02:48.87,0:02:52.03,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,Is anyone there?!\NSomebody, please, help me! Dialogue: 0,0:02:56.48,0:03:00.54,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,Kaworu should have been the one to survive. Dialogue: 0,0:03:01.38,0:03:04.44,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,He was much better than I am. Dialogue: 0,0:03:05.12,0:03:07.59,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,Kaworu should have survived. Dialogue: 0,0:03:07.59,0:03:11.39,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,No.\NA survivor needs only the will to live. Dialogue: 0,0:03:12.06,0:03:13.76,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,He wished for death. Dialogue: 0,0:03:13.76,0:03:16.66,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,He abandoned his will to live\Nand depended on false hope. Dialogue: 0,0:03:17.93,0:03:19.93,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,You weren't wrong, Shinji. Dialogue: 0,0:03:19.93,0:03:21.12,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,Is that really true? Dialogue: 0,0:03:21.12,0:03:21.79,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,ANXIETY Dialogue: 0,0:03:22.10,0:03:24.08,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,Was that what I wanted? Dialogue: 0,0:03:24.08,0:03:24.54,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,OBSESSION Dialogue: 0,0:03:24.84,0:03:27.71,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,I don't know.\NWhat can I do? Dialogue: 0,0:03:27.71,0:03:29.14,Default,,0000,0000,0000,,What am I supposed to do?!

[]: Re: Could Shinji kill Kaworu early in the series? - Yes. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Oct 04, 2011 12:38 am Azathoth wrote: my point is that not only does Shinji "believe" that Kaworu is an Angel and therefore entirely inhuman by nature, but that belief he's forced on himself is absolutely central to how he justifies killing Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Tue Oct 04, 2011 3:57 pm Am I the only one who would've found it more intelligent to have Leilel as the last angel, so that it would be three rapes in a row? Anyways, I would think Shinji could have killed Kaworu. Remember by the end of the series... All fellow pilots... weren't pilots really. Rei being a clone was just unveiled to Shinji. All his friends have left Tokyo-3. Considering these factors and that around the Bardiel fight, Shinji was at his high, although it would be difficult for him, he could have been capable of making Kaworu go bye bye.

[]:             Quote Post by Nivek » Tue Oct 04, 2011 6:48 pm I say yeah. He hadn't be come as desperate for friendship yet as he would later in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Oct 04, 2011 7:36 pm Stryker wrote: Am I the only one who would've found it more intelligent to have Leilel as the last angel, so that it would be three rapes in a row?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Oct 04, 2011 8:00 pm Azathoth wrote: Nah, he's gotta get the party started. Shinji is first in and last out as regards the rape pool.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Oct 04, 2011 8:19 pm Azathoth wrote: Anyway, I digress, because my point is that not only does Shinji "believe" that Kaworu is an Angel and therefore entirely inhuman by nature, but that belief he's forced on himself is absolutely central to how he justifies killing Kaworu. As long as he has that single justification that will allow him to push off his individual guilt and blame humanity in general, he certainly could have killed Kaworu earlier in the series. Hell, he could have killed Kaworu in the elementary-school sandbox and not given a shit.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Oct 04, 2011 8:22 pm I always thought of it as a “breaking point”, rather than a succession of rapes. Misato is broken by Kaji's death in episode 21; Asuka is broken by her rape in episode 22; Rei is left broken by her own rape, death, and revival; Shinji is broken by Kaworu's death at his hands.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Oct 05, 2011 4:08 pm CJD wrote: So you think the only reason Shinji had trouble with Bardiel was because he was emotionally stable prior to it?

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Oct 05, 2011 9:25 pm Stryker wrote: No, I don't think that. Had I suggested that? I don't mean to.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Oct 06, 2011 4:49 pm CJD wrote: Could Shinji kill Kaworu lacking character development earned after Bardiel?

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Fri Mar 02, 2012 3:17 pm TehDonutKing wrote: I always thought of it as a “breaking point”, rather than a succession of rapes. Misato is broken by Kaji's death in episode 21; Asuka is broken by her rape in episode 22; Rei is left broken by her own rape, death, and revival; Shinji is broken by Kaworu's death at his hands.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Mar 02, 2012 6:01 pm Agreed. Without the Bardiel incident and all the "bigger picture" conversations and determined actions that it led to I just don't think he'd have been able to squeeze. Heck, he almost wasn't able to do it AFTER all that, so before? No way.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Mar 02, 2012 8:06 pm NemZ wrote: Agreed. Without the Bardiel incident and all the "bigger picture" conversations and determined actions that it led to I just don't think he'd have been able to squeeze. Heck, he almost wasn't able to do it AFTER all that, so before? No way.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sun Mar 04, 2012 4:30 pm I think it's a really interesting question. I never connected the two events before, but they definitely parallel each other in a way.

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Sun Mar 04, 2012 7:28 pm chaosakita wrote: I think it's a really interesting question. I never connected the two events before, but they definitely parallel each other in a way.

[]: Shinji could NOT have killed Kaworu pre Bardiel             Quote Post by King Pen Pen of Tokyo 3 » Thu Aug 02, 2012 8:31 pm I don't believe Shinji would have been able to kill Kaworu (Tabris), had he not killed(Manga)/ Injured(Anime) Toji in his fight against Bardiel. First off what we all must remember Shinji had no idea who was in the cockpit of the EVA at the time. That being said what we also must remember is that Shinji loved Kaworu. Perhaps not in the way some Yaoi fan girls may believe, but he did have a strong relationship with Tabris (Kaworu). Now if he didn't have the heart to kill some he didn't know (because he didn't know Toji was in there at the time) he certainly would not have had the heart to kill some one he loved. In both cases he faced Angels. So one could not make the argument that he would have been able to kill Tabris pre Bardiel due to the fact that he was a threat, enemy, Angel. After his fight with Bardiel he began to toughen and close up emotionally. Being that things had become increasingly darker ever since episode 14. By the time we got to episode 24 he was (in my opinion) a completely different person from who he was when he faced Bardiel.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Aug 03, 2012 1:47 am I think one thing needs to be factored in which so far hasn't: Shinji's anger towards Kaworu. That's certainly what gets him into the fight. Emotion motivates him at that moment, not any lesson learned from Bardiel. Yes, he (mostly) doesn't want to kill Kaworu. Yes, he is conflicted. But surely part of him delights in getting back at his "betrayer".

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Aug 03, 2012 2:21 am Given how horrified and self-loathing he gets about the whole ordeal, down to claiming that Kaworu was the one who deserved to live and not him, I seriously doubt it.
